verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm-specific gamete adhesion/fusion factor with an Ig-like ectodomain and a transmembrane domain (single-pass TM protein). It is surface-exposed on sperm plasma membrane where it mediates sperm-egg binding and fusion during fertilization. However, its surface accessibility is restricted to the specialized context of spermatogenesis and the male gamete — it is not expressed on somatic cells accessible to systemic therapeutics. A binder could in principle access the protein on sperm in testicular or reproductive-tract settings, but not on typical cell-surface-therapy target tissues. This tissue/cell-type restriction and developmental specialization qualifies it as contextual rather than broadly accessible.
